LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NERDWALLET, INC.'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING FOLLOWS: GRANTED. ALL OF THE CLAIMS ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF TASHA AMARAL ARE COVERED BY THE PARTIES' ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. THIS CASE IS STAYED PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS. THERE IS SUFFICIENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT THE PARTIES AGREED TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT AND THAT AGREEMENT CALLED FOR THE APPLICATION OF JAMS EMPLOYMENT ARBITRATION RULES. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT HAS SOME MODICUM OF PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY, THE AGREEMENT IS NONETHELESS ENFORCEABLE BECAUSE IT IS NOT SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. THE ATTORNEYS' FEES PROVISION IS NOT SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE BECAUSE IT PRECLUDES A FEE AWARD PROHIBITED BY LAW. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT DOES NOT LACK MUTUALITY BECAUSE IT REQUIRES NERDWALLET TO ARBITRATE ALL EMPLOYMENT-RELATED DISPUTES AND WAS AGREED TO BY NERDWALLET BY THE VERY ACT OF GIVING IT TO MS. AMARAL. THE VENUE CLAUSE IS NOT SO ONEROUS AS TO BE SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. THE CONSULT LANGUAGE IS NEITHER MANDATORY NOR IMPERMISSIBLE AND, EVEN IF IT WAS, IT COULD BE SEVERED. NOR IS THERE ANY INDICATION THAT MS. AMARAL SOUGHT TO OR DID CONSULT WITH NERDWALLET'S COUNSEL. [ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING.] JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: LAURA A. REDING, CSR 	711, EMAIL: LREDING@SFTC.ORG
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
